pattern-model.summary: A Pattern is a pure function from a TimeSpan to a list of Events.
pattern-model.time: Rational arithmetic only. TimeSpan = { start, end } where start and end are rational numbers (numerator/denominator pairs). Never floating-point for time values.
pattern-model.cycle: The fundamental time unit. One cycle = one bar at the current BPM. All patterns repeat in cycles unless transformed.

event-shape.value: The payload: a keyword (:bd), a number (440), a map ({:note :c4 :amp 0.8}), or a rest keyword (:_).
event-shape.whole: TimeSpan covering the logical slot of the full event (e.g. one step in a seq).
event-shape.part: TimeSpan of the active slice — may be narrower than whole due to splitting.

pattern-model.purity: Pattern functions have no side-effects and produce identical output for the same TimeSpan. Audio output and DOM updates happen at the scheduler layer, not inside patterns.

value-types.keyword: Sample trigger or note name: :bd (bass drum), :sd (snare), :hh (hihat), :oh (open hihat). Arbitrary keywords look up samples by name in the active bank.
value-types.number: Hz frequency passed directly to synthesis: 440, 220.5. Combine with scale/chord to derive from degrees.
value-types.note-keyword: Pitch in equal temperament with A4=440 Hz. Format: :note[s|b]octave where s=sharp, b=flat. Examples: :c4 (middle C, 261.6 Hz), :fs3 (F#3), :bb5 (Bb5). Converted to Hz at eval time.
value-types.rest: :_ — silence. An event whose value is :_ is skipped by the audio scheduler.
value-types.map: Per-event parameter map merged into the event's metadata: {:note :c4 :amp 0.8 :decay 0.3 :pan -0.5}. Used by ->> and parameter transformers.
value-types.boolean: true / false — used in conditionals and control arguments like (midi-sync! true).
value-types.nil: null / absence. Many side-effecting built-ins return nil.

transformer-vs-pattern.pattern: A value that generates events when queried. (seq :bd :sd) is a Pattern. Patterns are the primary currency of REPuLse.
transformer-vs-pattern.transformer: A function Pattern → Pattern. (fast 2) with one argument returns a transformer. (amp 0.8) with one argument returns a transformer. Transformers compose with comp and chain with ->>.
transformer-vs-pattern.two-arg-form: Most transformers accept (f arg pat) — apply immediately — or (f arg) — return a transformer for ->> or comp.
transformer-vs-pattern.thread-last: (->> pat (amp 0.8) (attack 0.02) (decay 0.3)) threads pat as the last argument of each step. The natural way to apply a sequence of parameter transforms.

threading.->>: Thread-last macro. (->> x (f a) (g b)) expands to (g b (f a x)). Use for chaining pattern transformers.
threading.->: Thread-first macro. (-> x (f a) (g b)) expands to (g (f x a) b). Less common in REPuLse; ->> is preferred for pattern pipelines.
threading.comp: (comp f g …) composes transformers right-to-left. Useful for building named preset transformers: (def pluck (comp (amp 0.8) (decay 0.15) (attack 0.003))).

mini-notation.syntax: Space-separated tokens inside a string passed to (~): (~ "bd sd [hh hh] bd")

tokens.space: Sequence: 'bd sd hh oh' plays four events in equal steps.
tokens.[…]: Subdivision: [hh hh] shares one parent step, each sub-event gets half the time.
tokens.*N: Repeat: 'bd*4' expands to four bd events in one step.
tokens.<…>: Alternation: <bd sd> plays bd on cycle 0, sd on cycle 1, cycling. Equivalent to (alt …).
tokens.?: 50% probability: 'bd?' plays bd roughly half the time.
tokens.:N: Sample index: 'bd:2' selects the third sample in the bd bank.
tokens.@N: Weight: 'bd@2 sd' gives bd twice the time slot of sd.

track-model.named-tracks: (track :name pattern) registers a pattern under a keyword name. Multiple tracks run in parallel, each queried independently by the scheduler.
track-model.anonymous-track: A bare top-level pattern (not wrapped in track) runs on the implicit anonymous track. Only one anonymous track exists at a time.
track-model.mute: (mute! :name) silences a track without removing it from the scheduler. (unmute! :name) re-enables. (solo! :name) mutes all other tracks.
track-model.update: (upd) re-evaluates the editor buffer and hot-swaps running tracks without restarting the scheduler or producing audible glitches.
track-model.clear: (clear! :name) removes a specific track. (clear!) removes all tracks. Stopping audio entirely: (stop).

effects.global-fx: (fx :reverb 0.4) sets a master-chain effect parameter. Global effects persist across evaluations.
effects.per-track-fx: (->> pat (fx :filter 1000)) routes a pattern through a per-track effect chain. Re-evaluating code that omits the fx call removes the effect.

synthesis.sample-playback: Default audio output. Keywords like :bd look up WAV samples in the active bank.
synthesis.synth-voices: (synth :saw pat) applies a built-in oscillator voice. Available voices: :saw, :square, :fm. Alternatively: (saw :c4), (square :c4), (fm :c4 :index 3).
synthesis.defsynth: (defsynth name [params] body) defines a custom synthesizer using Web Audio UGens: sin, tri, saw, lpf, hpf, bpf, mix, gain, env-perc, env-asr, env-gen, bus, out, in.
synthesis.ugen-graph: UGens are composable audio-graph nodes. (->> (sin freq) (lpf 2000) (env-perc 0.01 0.3)) wires a sine oscillator through a filter and percussive envelope.

time-control.bpm: (bpm n) sets the global tempo. Patterns are time-mapped to this tempo. Range: 20–400 BPM.
time-control.fast-slow: (fast 2 pat) doubles the playback rate within a cycle. (slow 2 pat) halves it. These are cycle-relative — fast 2 plays twice as many events per cycle.
time-control.late-early: (late 0.25 pat) shifts all events forward by 0.25 cycles. (early 0.25 pat) shifts backward.
time-control.tween: (tween :linear start end bars) produces a smoothly interpolated value over time. Use as an argument to amp, attack, etc. Transition is per-sample in the WASM engine.

music-theory.scale: (scale :minor :c4 (seq 0 2 4 7)) maps degree integers to Hz using a named scale. Degree 0 is the root. Negative degrees and degrees > scale-length wrap into adjacent octaves.
music-theory.chord: (chord :major7 :c4) stacks all tones of the named chord as simultaneous Hz values in a pattern.
music-theory.transpose: (transpose n pat) shifts all Hz values by n semitones. Keywords that are not note-keywords (e.g. :bd, :_) pass through unchanged.

session-snapshot.description: The output of (help-export) — a plain JS object suitable for pasting into an AI prompt or external tool.

fields.bpm: Current tempo in beats per minute (number).
fields.tracks: Active track names as an object with boolean true values. Patterns are stripped — only names are included.
fields.muted: Array of muted track name strings.
fields.fx: Array of global effect objects: { name, params, bypassed }.
fields.bank: Active bank prefix string or null.
fields.sources: Array of loaded external sample sources: { type, id }.

session-snapshot.privacy: Editor buffer text (def bodies, pattern code) is intentionally omitted. Copy-paste from the editor if you want to share code.
